items.text: Free yoga in the quad this Saturday at 9 AM! Bring your own mat or grab one for $3 at the table. Best way to start the weekend, honestly. All levels welcome — Maria's an amazing instructor!

questions.stem: According to the post, what do students need to do if they have no mat?

options.A: Pay a small charge at the table
options.B: Reserve a spot in advance
options.D: Arrive thirty minutes earlier

questions.explanation: The post says you can grab a mat for $3 at the table, so students without a mat pay a small charge.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the yoga session?

options.A: It requires prior yoga experience
options.B: It suits beginners and experienced people
options.C: It is limited to a small group
options.D: It moves indoors during bad weather

questions.explanation: Because the post states all levels are welcome, it can be inferred the session suits both beginners and experienced participants.

items.text: Dear Jordan, Our records show your meal plan ended with a $42.50 balance. We'll refund this amount to your student account by June 10. No action is needed on your part. Best regards, Dining Services

questions.stem: According to the email, where will the money be sent?

options.A: To a personal bank account
options.B: To a prepaid dining card
options.D: To the student's school account

questions.explanation: The email states the balance will be refunded to the student's account, matching the school account option.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the recipient's next step?

options.A: They should reply to confirm the amount
options.B: They must visit the dining office soon
options.C: They can simply wait for the refund
options.D: They need to submit a refund request

questions.explanation: Since the email says no action is needed, it can be inferred the recipient can simply wait for the refund to arrive.

items.text: Hi Priya, The book you requested through interlibrary loan has arrived. Please pick it up at the Circulation Desk by May 22. After that date, it will be returned to the lending library. Thanks, Library Staff

questions.stem: According to the email, where should the student collect the book?

options.A: At the Circulation Desk
options.B: At the reference section
options.D: At the front entrance

questions.explanation: The email directs the student to pick up the book at the Circulation Desk.

questions.stem: What can be inferred if the student waits past May 22?

options.A: The student will owe a daily fine
options.B: The student must request it again
options.C: The student will lose access to it
options.D: The student can collect it later

questions.explanation: Since the book returns to the lending library after May 22, it can be inferred the student would lose access to it.

items.text: Hi Sam, Your replacement student ID is ready. Please stop by the ID Office in Room 110 to collect it. There's a $10 fee, payable by card only. Bring a photo ID to verify your identity. Thanks, Campus Services

questions.stem: According to the email, how must the student pay the fee?

options.A: Using cash at the desk
options.B: Using a card only
options.D: Using a personal check

questions.explanation: The email specifies that the $10 fee is payable by card only.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about collecting the new ID?

options.A: It can be mailed to the student
options.B: It requires an advance appointment
options.C: It will be ready next week
options.D: It must be picked up in person

questions.explanation: Since the student is told to stop by the office and bring photo ID, it can be inferred the new ID must be picked up in person.

items.text: ATTENTION: The third-floor study lounge in Baker Hall will be closed for repainting on March 8-9. Students may use the second-floor lounge during this period. The space will reopen at 8:00 AM on March 10. Thank you for your patience.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what should students use during the closure?

options.A: A lounge on a lower floor
options.B: A reserved seat in the library
options.D: An outdoor study terrace

questions.explanation: The notice tells students to use the second-floor lounge, which is a lounge on a lower floor.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the lounge on March 10?

options.A: It will remain partly closed
options.B: It will require a reservation
options.D: It will charge an entry fee

questions.explanation: Since the notice says the space reopens at 8:00 AM on March 10, it can be inferred it will be available for use again.

items.text: NOTICE: Parking in South Lot C is reserved for permit holders from 7:00 AM to 6:00 PM on weekdays. Vehicles without a valid permit will be ticketed. Visitors may park in Lot D free of charge. Questions? Contact the Parking Office.

questions.stem: According to the notice, where may campus visitors leave their cars?

options.A: In a different marked lot
options.B: In any open street spot
options.D: In the staff garage level

questions.explanation: The notice states visitors may park in Lot D, which is a different marked lot.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about parking in South Lot C on Sunday?

options.A: A permit is still mandatory
options.B: Tickets are issued more often
options.D: The lot is completely closed

questions.explanation: Since the permit rule applies only on weekdays, it can be inferred the restriction does not apply on Sunday.

items.text: USED TEXTBOOKS FOR SALE! Selling biology and chemistry texts, gently used, $15 each. Meet at the Student Union lobby this Thursday after 3 PM. Cash only. Great condition — perfect for next semester. Text Dana at the number below to claim yours!

questions.stem: According to the ad, when will the seller meet buyers?

options.A: On a weekday afternoon
options.B: On an early Friday morning
options.D: On a Monday lunch break

questions.explanation: The ad says to meet this Thursday after 3 PM, which is a weekday afternoon.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about paying for a textbook?

options.A: A card reader is provided
options.B: A deposit must be sent first
options.D: Physical money is required

questions.explanation: Since the ad states cash only, it can be inferred that physical money is required to buy a textbook.

items.text: Writing Center Spring Hours: Monday-Thursday, 10:00 AM to 7:00 PM; Friday, 10:00 AM to 3:00 PM. Closed weekends. Walk-ins welcome, but appointments are recommended for busy weeks. Book online or call Ms. Reyes at the front desk to reserve a slot.

questions.stem: According to the schedule, what is recommended during busy periods?

options.A: Booking a session ahead of time
options.B: Arriving right when doors open
options.D: Visiting only on weekday mornings

questions.explanation: The schedule recommends appointments for busy weeks, which means booking a session ahead of time.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about visiting the center on Saturday?

options.A: It opens for a shorter time
options.B: It needs an early reservation
options.D: It will not be available

questions.explanation: Since the schedule says the center is closed on weekends, it can be inferred it will not be available on Saturday.

items.text:
The Henderson Library will undergo scheduled maintenance on Saturday, March 14, from 8:00 AM to 2:00 PM. During this period, the second and third floors will be closed for electrical upgrades. The main entrance and ground-floor study commons will remain open throughout.

Students are reminded of the following:

- All laptops and personal belongings must be removed from the upper floors by Friday, March 13, at 5:00 PM.
- Items left behind will be relocated to the Lost and Found desk in Room 110.
- Printing services on the third floor will be temporarily unavailable; please use the ground-floor printers near the Help Desk.
- A $5.00 retrieval fee applies to any belongings collected after March 20.

Reserved group study rooms scheduled during the maintenance window have been automatically cancelled. Affected students will receive a confirmation email and may rebook through the library portal once service resumes. For questions, please contact the Circulation Office at circ@henderson.edu or visit Room 110 during regular hours.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this notice?

options.A: To explain how students can reserve group study rooms online
options.B: To inform students about a temporary library closure for maintenance
options.C: To announce the permanent relocation of printing services
options.D: To describe new electrical safety rules for the entire campus

questions.explanation: The notice synthesizes information across the text: it announces scheduled maintenance, the closure of the upper floors, and the temporary unavailability of services. The overall purpose is to inform students about the temporary closure, not to explain reservations (A, a detail), announce a permanent change (C, contradicted by 'temporarily unavailable'), or describe campus-wide rules (D, too broad).

questions.stem: According to the notice, what must students do by Friday, March 13?

options.A: Rebook their group study rooms through the portal
options.B: Contact the Circulation Office about printing services
options.C: Remove their belongings from the upper floors
options.D: Collect any lost items from the Help Desk

questions.explanation: The notice states that 'all laptops and personal belongings must be removed from the upper floors by Friday, March 13, at 5:00 PM.' Option A reverses the timeline (rebooking happens after service resumes). Option B borrows 'Circulation Office' and 'printing' but combines them wrongly. Option D swaps the Lost and Found in Room 110 with the Help Desk.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about a student who retrieves a left-behind laptop on March 22?

options.A: They will rebook the room through the library portal
options.B: They will be charged a retrieval fee
options.C: They will receive a confirmation email
options.D: They will use the ground-floor printers

questions.explanation: The notice states a '$5.00 retrieval fee applies to any belongings collected after March 20.' Since March 22 is after that date, it can be inferred the student will be charged. Option A applies to cancelled study rooms, not belongings. Option C refers to students with cancelled reservations. Option D refers to printing, an unrelated service.

items.text:
Subject: Updated Parking Policy for Fall Semester

Dear Residents of Wexford Hall,

I'm writing to let you know about important changes to the campus parking policy that take effect on September 1. These updates are designed to improve safety and ensure fair access to the limited spaces in Lot C.

Starting this fall, all resident vehicles must display a valid Lot C permit, which costs $45 per semester. Permits can be purchased online through the student portal and must be renewed each term. Vehicles parked in Lot C without a visible permit after September 8 will receive a $25 citation.

Please note the following:

- Overnight parking is permitted only for registered residents.
- Visitors must obtain a temporary pass from the front desk in Wexford Hall.
- The lot will be closed for resurfacing from September 20 to September 22.

If you have already registered your vehicle, you don't need to take any further action. For other questions, please reach out to me directly at parking@wexford.edu.

Best regards,
Daniel Reyes
Residence Parking Coordinator

format_metadata.subject: Updated Parking Policy for Fall Semester

questions.stem: According to the email, what must residents do to park in Lot C this fall?

options.A: Display a valid permit costing $45 per semester
options.B: Register their vehicle at the front desk each week
options.C: Obtain a temporary pass from the residence coordinator
options.D: Renew their citation through the online student portal

questions.explanation: The email states that 'all resident vehicles must display a valid Lot C permit, which costs $45 per semester.' Option B borrows 'front desk' and 'register' but the front desk is for visitor passes. Option C swaps the permit with the visitor temporary pass. Option D wrongly attaches 'renew' and 'portal' to a citation rather than a permit.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about a resident who parks in Lot C without a permit on September 10?

options.A: They will need to purchase a temporary visitor pass
options.B: They will have their vehicle registered automatically
options.C: They will be required to pay a citation
options.D: They will be allowed overnight parking access

questions.explanation: The email states that vehicles without a visible permit 'after September 8 will receive a $25 citation.' Since September 10 is after that date, the resident will be required to pay. Option A applies to visitors, not residents. Option B reverses the policy, which requires action from the resident. Option D contradicts the rule, as overnight parking requires registration.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this email?

options.A: To remind residents to renew their visitor passes online
options.B: To announce changes to the campus parking policy
options.C: To explain why Lot C will be permanently closed
options.D: To request that residents register at the front desk

questions.explanation: The email synthesizes several updates—permits, citations, and closures—under the stated goal of informing residents about 'important changes to the campus parking policy.' Option A captures only a minor detail and misattributes renewal to visitor passes. Option C contradicts the temporary resurfacing dates. Option D is too narrow and applies to visitors.

questions.stem: According to the email, when will Lot C be closed for resurfacing?

options.A: From September 1 through the end of the term
options.B: From September 8 until permits are renewed
options.D: From September 22 until the front desk reopens

questions.explanation: The email states that 'the lot will be closed for resurfacing from September 20 to September 22.' Option A swaps in the policy start date (September 1). Option B uses the citation date (September 8) in the wrong context. Option D reverses the end date and attaches an unrelated front desk condition.
